column1: # templates/archetypes.tsv — the CANONICAL PROJECT ARCHETYPES (HERD-409): the committed,
# authoritative axis of PROJECT KIND, orthogonal to the operating postures in templates/postures.tsv
# (postures answer "who merges / does a human approve"; archetypes answer "what IS this project" —
# a codebase with a test suite, or a docs/research repo with none). `herd init` (bin/herd) offers the
# named archetypes here at init time, records the choice as the validated PROJECT_ARCHETYPE key in
# .herd/config, and consults it to pick the seeded healthcheck template + frame the health/preview
# interview questions. Keep it authoritative: add an archetype here (not hardcoded in bin/herd) and
# every consumer picks it up.
# Grounding (HERD-409): a non-code project ran `herd init`; scout correctly reported
# language=unknown, but init still seeded the Python-test-suite EXAMPLE healthcheck and the
# interview assumed a test/lint/app-preview shape — there was no way to say "this project has no
# test suite to run."
# Format — one TAB-SEPARATED row per archetype (columns separated by a literal tab, NOT spaces):
#     name <TAB> healthcheck_template <TAB> intent
#   name                 kebab-case archetype id — stable; the PROJECT_ARCHETYPE config value.
#   healthcheck_template the templates/healthcheck.*.sh basename `herd init` seeds
#                         .herd/healthcheck.project.sh from for this archetype, OR the literal
#                         token "auto" meaning "defer to scout's detected language" (the `code`
#                         archetype's stack-aware behavior, unchanged since before HERD-409).
#   intent                one-line human description of what kind of project this is FOR.
# Blank lines and lines whose first field starts with '#' are ignored; the columns are tab-separated.
name
code
column3: Software project: a test/lint suite exists to run. Seeded healthcheck is chosen from scout's detected language (Go/Rust/Java/Node/Python) via the stack-aware templates — the default archetype, byte-identical to pre-HERD-409 init.

column1: research-lab
column3: Research/experiment repo: no runnable app or test suite. Seeded healthcheck lints markdown docs (fenced-block + relative-link + template-conformance checks) instead of running a test suite; the interview skips app-preview framing.

column1: docs
column3: Documentation-only project: same markdown/link/template-conformance healthcheck as research-lab; the interview frames the health question around doc linting, not a test/lint/app-preview stack.
